title: Motor Vehicle Production (Total & Passenger Cars), CBI/SAPCO/MIMT Annual Series
primary_source: cbi-iran-annual-review (SAPCO through 2018/19, Ministry of Industry Mine & Trade from 2019/20)
notes: Picks up almost exactly where the existing chart's last real (non-milestone) data point (2005, 817,200 units, sourced to Wikipedia) leaves off, and upgrades the source class for this whole era from secondary (Wikipedia) to primary Iranian government (CBI Annual Review, itself citing SAPCO / Ministry of Industry Mine and Trade). Two measures: total light+heavy vehicles manufactured (thousand units) and passenger-car sub-count (thousand units) -- kept as two variant series, not summed/conflated. Known gaps: SH1390 (2011/12) and SH1392 (2013/14) narratives do not state a plain total-vehicle figure in either edition checked; left blank rather than interpolated. Full derivation and per-year sourcing notes in data/processed/iran_industry_energy_enrich_series/README.md and motor_vehicle_production_cbi_2009_2022.csv.

title: Natural Gas Production (by Source) & Consumption (by Sector), 2001-2022
primary_source: sci-amar (production, Yearbook 1399 Ch.7 Table 7.7) | cbi-iran-annual-review (consumption, Table 10, sourced to NIGC)
notes: Same underlying concept as pahlavi__gas_production_consumption_1965_69 (production + consumption, both present here) but ~35-55 years later -- a genuine decade-plus continuation, not just a nearby-topic new chart. Also directly extends the already-registered iran_energy__natural_gas_consumption_1370_1385 chart (1991-2006, gap years) for the consumption slice specifically: this dataset's consumption coverage starts 2005 and runs to 2022, overlapping 2005-2006 with that chart for cross-validation and picking up every year after where it stops. Production-side data (SCI Yearbook Table 7.7, source: Ministry of Oil) is sparser (6 data points, 2001-2017) since it comes from a single yearbook edition's multi-year table rather than a stacked union of many editions. Consumption breakdown is by END-USE SECTOR (residential+commercial+industrial / power plants / major industries / transportation), a different axis from the 1965-69 chart's flared/consumed/injected framing -- kept as distinct variant series, not force-mapped onto the older categories.

title: Domestic Consumption of Oil Products by Type (Gas Oil, Fuel Oil, Gasoline, Kerosene, LPG)
primary_source: cbi-iran-annual-review (Table 8, sourced to Ministry of Petroleum)
notes: DOMESTIC CONSUMPTION (thousand barrels/day) by product type -- a different measure from the already-registered iran_energy__oil_products_imports_1370_1385 chart (which tracks IMPORT volumes, not consumption; 1991-2006 gap years only), so staged as new rather than extends. Genuine reporting gap from 2018/19 onward: confirmed present as '..' (not available) in both the newest (2022/23) and second-newest (2021/22) CBI Annual Review editions for this exact table, i.e. Ministry of Petroleum appears to have stopped publishing this breakdown in CBI's appendix, not a retrieval failure on this project's part. See README caveats on the parsing method (automated regex extraction across 23 PDF editions, spot-checked).

title: Average Oil Product Production by Refineries, by Type
primary_source: sci-amar (Statistical Yearbook 1399, Ch.7 Table 7.3, sourced to Ministry of Oil)
time_range: 2001,2006,2011,2015,2016,2017 SH1380/1385/1390/1394/1395/1396 (sparse -- single yearbook edition's multi-year table, not a stacked annual union)
notes: PRODUCTION (cu m/day, average) at Iranian refineries by product type -- distinct from the consumption chart above and from the existing oil_products_imports chart (imports). No existing chart tracks this measure. jet_fuels/lubricants/bitumen/other_products/total rows only populated for 2001 and 2006 in the source table itself (blank for 1390 SH onward in the original PDF, not a transcription gap -- see README). Only a single yearbook edition was mined for this table (unlike the CBI tables, which stack 23 editions); a follow-up pass pulling Chapter 7 from additional yearbook editions (1388-1398 SH are all already identified and Wayback-archived, see logs) could plausibly fill this to a fully annual series the same way the CBI oil/gas consumption tables were densified this session -- flagged as a concrete next step, not attempted this round due to time-boxing.
